business.headline: BNW's Smita Nair sees strong demand for UAE luxury homesSpeaking at a session at the India Today Conclave, Nair said demand for ultra-luxury and branded residences remains strong even as parts of the mid-market segment show caution.

Luxury, waterfront homes driving UAE real estate demand: BNW's Smita NairSpeaking at a breakaway session at the India Today Conclave 2026, Smita Nair said demand for ultra-luxury and branded residences remains strong, particularly among international investors.

Small teams will soon disrupt big companies in AI era: Salim IsmailSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, the founder of OpenExO and founding executive director of Singularity University said rapid advances in AI are changing how companies operate and compete.

The next $1 trillion company could have only 5 employees: Singularity's Salim IsmailSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Salim Ismail, founding executive director of Singularity University and founder of OpenExO, said the rapid acceleration of technologies such as artificial intelligence is changing the economics of innovation and giving small teams unprecedented power to disrupt large corporations.

India a bright spot amid global uncertainty, says AT Capital's Arvind TikuIndia's large population, strong domestic demand and long-term infrastructure needs make it one of the most promising markets globally, says Arvind Tiku.
Renewables, urbanisation and demand to power India's growth: AT Capital's Arvind TikuIndia's large domestic market and young population create strong long-term growth potential, says Arvind Tiku.

If valuables vanish from your locker, can insurance cover the loss?This is the second story in Lock & Key, a series examining how India's bank locker system works and what customers should know before trusting their valuables to a steel vault.

Either I collapse or get up and go: Anil Agarwal reflects on loss of his sonVedanta chairman Anil Agarwal said the death of his son Agnivesh has strengthened his resolve to continue working for India, urging opportunities for youth and calling for world-class education at home.

ED arrests 2 in Rs 183-crore online ponzi scam; accused returned from DubaiThe case was registered in December 2024 in Thane, accusing Sudhir Kotadiya, Umang Kotadiya, Jaysukh Sakhariya and others of running a fraudulent digital investment scheme that allegedly collected large sums from investors in Gujarat and Maharashtra.

Highlights: Two vessels carrying 92,700 metric tons of LPG en route to India, govt assuresLPG Crisis Highlights: Amid reports of commercial LPG cylinder shortage, Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge slammed the Centre, calling the crisis a result of the BJP government's "disastrous policy" and questioned PM Narendra Modi's foreign policy moves. Meanwhile, the shortage is beginning to affect services on the ground as well—food for the public at the "Didi Ki Rasoi" run by Jeevika didis at Rohtas Sadar Hospital has been stopped due to lack of LPG cylinders, and patient meals could also be affected if supply is not restored. Meanwhile, the government has assured that two vessels named Nanda Devi and Shivalik are en route to India with 92,700 metric tons of LPG cylinders.

Which four shipping routes control global trade? Karan Adani breaks it downKaran Adani explained that a large part of global shipping passes through four critical choke points. These routes act as key gateways for international trade and energy transport.

How is the global energy landscape changing? Karan Adani explainsSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Karan Adani said the world has witnessed repeated disruptions in supply chains over the past few years, forcing countries to rethink how they manage energy and trade.

After Air India, IndiGo imposes fuel surcharge amid Middle East crisisThe energy crisis triggered by the ongoing war in the Middle East is all set to make your air travel costlier. India's biggest domestic airline, IndiGo, on Friday announced it will impose Rs 425 to Rs 2,300 fuel surcharge on domestic and international flights from Friday (March 14).

Energy, ports and materials define Adani Group's strategy: Karan AdaniKaran Adani emphasised that the group's business expansion is not random but guided by long-term economic changes. Energy transition, digital infrastructure and industrial materials are areas expected to grow significantly in the coming years.

Adani Ports ready to handle energy cargo amid global disruptions: Karan AdaniSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Karan Adani, Managing Director of Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd said supply chains worldwide have faced repeated shocks in recent years, forcing countries to rethink their dependence on highly globalised trade networks.

In an AI-driven world, storytelling and creativity will matter most: Christina WallaceWallace spoke about the growing impact of artificial intelligence. While AI will change many jobs, she believes human creativity will remain essential.

Storytelling will be the most valuable skill in the AI era: Christina WallaceOne skill that Wallace believes will become even more important in the age of AI is storytelling. While machines can produce data and structured information, they struggle to capture human emotion and experiences.

IndiGo, Air India seek tax relief as airspace bans hit operations, raise costIndiGo and Air India have urged the government to cut aviation fuel taxes and reduce private airport charges as the Iran conflict and Pakistan airspace ban force longer routes, raising operational costs and straining airline finances.
India's bond market must expand to finance Viksit Bharat goals, says Anurag MittalIndia will require massive investment to sustain growth, says Mittal.

India needs stronger bond market to fund Viksit Bharat vision, says Anurag MittalMittal explained that while India's growth story has been strong, future development will need long-term capital from a wider range of investors, not just government spending.

entertainment.headline: Ustaad Bhagat Singh trailer: Pawan Kalyan vows real violence, mass drama The trailer of Ustaad Bhagat Singh starring Pawan Kalyan is finally out. The film is set to clash with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge at the box office.

Harry Styles claps back at queerbaiting criticism with a kiss on Saturday Night Live During his recent Saturday Night Live appearance, Harry Styles used humor and direct engagement to address ongoing queerbaiting allegations. The singer proved his point by kissing Ben Marshall on stage.

Chinmayi questions Rajinikanth, Kamal Haasan for praising Vairamuthu's Jnanpith win Singer Chinmayi Sripaada, who named lyricist Vairamuthu during the MeToo movement, has called out actors Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan for their public praise of the veteran poet after he was selected for the Jnanpith Award.

Ranveer Singh, Deepika attend Eka Lakhani wedding amid Dhurandhar 2 hype Ranveer Singh and Deepika Padukone attended celebrity stylist Eka Lakhani's wedding with Ravi Bhagchandka on March 14. A picture of the couple posing with a guest from the festivities has now surfaced online.

Most shocking Oscar moments ever; see them here The Oscars have always been a blend of glamour and unexpected drama, with moments ranging from shocking attacks to wardrobe malfunctions. Such incidents, including Will Smith's infamous slap and the Envelopegate blunder, continue to be fresh in audience memory even as they shape Oscar legacy.

80,000 watched Anurag Dobhal 'final drive': What it says about cost of living online After YouTuber Anurag Dobhal crashed his car during a livestream watched by thousands, questions surfaced about the pressure of living life online. From creators like Armaan Malik to Kusha Kapila, the influencer economy is forcing a deeper conversation about the personal cost of constant visibility.

Watch: Aamir Khan cuts birthday cake with near ones, Irfan Pathan shares video Actor Aamir Khan celebrated his 61st birthday on March 14 with an intimate bash attended by his partner Gauri Spratt, children Junaid, Ira and Azaad, and close friends. A video from the party has emerged online.

Akshay Kumar cites Dhurandhar to explain Bollywood's changing trends Bollywood actor Akshay Kumar highlighted the evolving trends in Indian cinema, noting the renewed popularity of high-energy action films like Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar. He emphasised how audience preferences cycle through genres.

Akshay Kumar reveals why dinner talk with wife Twinkle has moved beyond politics At the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Akshay Kumar spoke about dinner table conversation at home with wife Twinkle Khanna. The actor shared their discussions no longer don't include politics, and why.

Flow like water: Akshay Kumar talks about surviving Bollywood's highs and lows Akshay Kumar shared insights on his disciplined approach to managing four films a year and maintaining high energy through effective time management. He also spoke about his early morning routine and love for comedy.

He is gold: KJo impressed by Anil Kapoor's performance in Subedaar. See post Karan Johar gave a shout-out to Anil Kapoor's performance in the recent release Subedaar. The movie is currently streaming on Prime Video.

Akshay Kumar joins Golmaal 5; sports bald new look in BTS clip. See here Akshay Kumar announced his entry into the Golmaal franchise with the upcoming fifth instalment in a 'bald' new avatar.  The actor shared a photo with a major update from the film's sets. Read all about it.

Lyricist-poet Vairamuthu wins Jnanpith Award, third Tamil recipient in 20 years Acclaimed poet and lyricist Vairamuthu has been awarded the Jnanpith Award for his contribution to Indian literature. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin shared a post on social media and congratulated the stalwart.

There can be only one Aamir: Saira Banu's touching note for actor on 61st birthday Veteran actor Saira Banu posted a heartfelt message for Aamir Khan on his birthday, highlighting his personal bond with her family and longstanding admiration for Dilip Kumar.

Farah Khan reveals reason why she once avoided working with Akshaye Khanna Farah Khan shared that she once avoided working with Akshaye Khanna in the 1990s. Read here what she said, and the opinion she arrived at after they collaborated on the 2001 hit, Dil Chahta Hai.

Watch: Alia-Ranbir enjoy family time with Raha in Hong Kong park Alia Bhatt is set to celebrate her 33rd birthday with Ranbir Kapoor and Raha in Hong Kong. Several videos and pictures of the trio enjoying family time in a park have emerged online.

Don't call us foreigners: Arunachal girl band Gilithigreams on outsiders label Gilithigreams, a girl pop band from Arunachal Pradesh, regaled guests at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, and urged people not to address Northeasterners as foreigners.

Sunidhi Chauhan postpones Kolkata concert on medical advice. Read statement Sunidhi Chauhan has postponed her Kolkata show, citing a throat infection. In a post on social media, she said her doctors have advised her to rest.

Rajkummar Rao and Keerthy Suresh team up for start-up drama Raftaar, release date out Rajkummar Rao and Keerthy Suresh have announced their upcoming film Raftaar, which is set to release in theatres on July 24, 2026.

general news.headline: BJP offers Deputy CM post to Vijay, 80 seats to TVK for Tamil Nadu polls: SourcesTalks between the BJP and Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) have entered the final stage ahead of the Tamil Nadu Assembly polls, with the BJP offering 80 seats and the Deputy CM post to the actor-politician, sources said.

Rs 500-hike in allowances of priests, muezzins: Bengal CM's last minute poll sopThe announcement came ahead of the enforcement of the Model Code of Conduct, which comes into effect once the poll schedule is declared.

Iran vows to pursue, kill 'child killer' Netanyahu amid assassination rumours  Live UpdatesThe statement also delivered a direct warning to Netanyahu, saying that if the Israeli prime minister is "still alive," Iran will keep hunting him down and "kill him with full force."

Poll dates for Bengal, Kerala, Puducherry, 2 other states to be announced todayThe Election Commission will announce poll dates for Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, Kerala, Assam and the Union Territory of Puducherry in a press conference at 4 pm.

The Hands of God that shaped Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup redemptionSanju Samson began the year battling doubt and uncertainty. Months later, he stood as India's T20 World Cup hero, lifted by belief, mentorship and a support system that helped him rediscover the fearless batter within.

Why is it suddenly raining in Delhi in March amid rising heat?A chain of western disturbances brought unexpected rain, thunderstorms, and cooler temperatures to Delhi and northern India on March 15, 2026. Weather experts say India may avoid a severe heatwave until mid-to-late April, as spring makes a surprise return.

Netanyahu's office rebuts rumours of his death, says Israeli PM is fineAmid the Middle East war, Benjamin Netanyahu's office has rejected viral claims that the Israeli Prime Minister was killed after online speculation grew over an alleged AI-generated video of his recent address.

An AI-proof, recession-proof career, with blessings from templesThere is seemingly a job that AI can't take away, and it might boom during recession. Wondering what it is? It's related to India's temple economy, and is coming up as a new career path. Courses in temple management are drawing Gen Z students and retirees alike.

Meet Maryam Mirzakhani, Iranian who became first woman to win Nobel Prize of mathsGrowing up in Tehran, Maryam Mirzakhani once dreamed of becoming a writer. But in 2014, she made history by winning the Fields Medal, becoming the first woman to receive mathematics' highest honour.

Another strait to get shut after Hormuz? Houthis ready to pull trigger in Iran warWhile the Strait of Hormuz is choked amid the war on Iran, the focus is now on another strait in the region, the Bab el-Mandeb. The Yemeni Houthi group, part of Tehran's "Axis of Resistance", has warned its "fingers are on the trigger". The Houthis joining the war could choke yet another crucial artery of global trade.

When wars entered the kitchen: How global conflicts changed what we eatHandcrafted ByOur Editors From canned food to instant coffee, many everyday staples were born not in kitchens but on battlefields.

'I lived without UPI and used only cash until 2025. Here's how I survived'Handcrafted ByOur Editors We bring you the story of a 31-year-old HR professional who managed without UPI, well almost, until 2025 in a city like Delhi, where people often ask, "Who uses cash now?" even for routine payments.

Most shocking Oscar moments ever; see them hereThe Oscars have always been a blend of glamour and unexpected drama, with moments ranging from shocking attacks to wardrobe malfunctions. Such incidents, including Will Smith's infamous slap and the Envelopegate blunder, continue to be fresh in audience memory even as they shape Oscar legacy.

CBSE cancels Class 12 board exams in Middle East amid US-Israel-Iran warThe Central Board of Secondary Education, CBSE, has cancelled the Class 12 board examinations in several Middle Eastern countries due to the ongoing US-Israel-Iran war. The board also confirmed that the mechanism for announcing results for affected students will be announced at a later stage.

I'm a proud grihini: Influencer argues homemaking is a choice, not a compromiseIn a world where hustle is romanticised, where women are expected to juggle home and work, Ragini Mathur chose to stop and smell the roses. She enjoys being a grihini because it is her choice, and she believes the societal perspective towards a housewife needs to change.

Work without pause: Have weekend emails and 70-hour weeks become normal?Handcrafted ByOur Editors From late-night emails to calls for 70-hour work schedules, the rise of hustle culture and always-on workplaces is reshaping professional expectations, raising growing concerns about employee burnout, long working hours and work-life balance.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!
Accord, Acrimony, Assaults: MGR Leaves, Two Leaves Wither & Sun Rises | Part 33

Starlink launches in Kuwait amid US-Israel-Iran warElon Musk's satellite internet service, Starlink, is now available in Kuwait. This announcement comes at a time when the country has found itself impacted by the US-Israel war with Iran.

Techie uses ChatGPT and AlphaFold to build DIY mRNA cancer vaccine, saves dogAustralian tech entrepreneur Paul Conyngham claims that he used AI tools such as ChatGPT and AlphaFold to cure his dog's cancer by making custom mRNA vaccines after traditional treatment failed to stop the tumour.

India is re-engineering its grains. The lab is finally serving the kitchenA silent revolution is unfolding inside Indian laboratories. From the patent-free power of an indigenous CRISPR-like tool to gene-edited Kamala rice and high-protein designer grains, Indian scientists are rewriting the very architecture of food to feed 1.4 billion people.

Will hit it just for fun: Trump warns of fresh US strikes on Iran's Kharg IslandThe US president has indicated the possibility of further US military action against oil hub Iran's Kharg Island, emphasising that while Iran signals readiness for talks, current terms remain unsatisfactory.

The real reason you want sugar after meals, according to a doctorMany people blame themselves for craving sweets, but doctors say it is not simply about willpower. Hormones, blood sugar levels, stress, and sleep patterns all play a role in triggering sugar cravings.

sports.headline: F1 Shanghai GP: Mercedes' Antonelli takes first win, Hamilton secures first podium Kimi Antonelli won the 2026 Chinese Grand Prix for Mercedes, with Lewis Hamilton taking his first Ferrari podium. McLaren's Norris and Piastri both failed to start in Shanghai.

KKR should not have let Shreyas Iyer go: Anil Kumble slams team's retention policy Anil Kumble believes Kolkata Knight Riders weakened their own title-winning core by releasing Shreyas Iyer and Phil Salt after their 2024 triumph. The former India captain criticised the franchise's inconsistent retention strategy.

New kit in 7 hours: Indian football body hits back at critics over Women's Asian Cup row AIFF deputy secretary general M Satyanarayan says the federation replaced the Indian women's team's ill-fitting AFC Women's Asian Cup kits within seven hours, but the effort went unrecognised.

Robot, national treasure: Suryakumar doesn't hold back on his praise for Bumrah T20 World Cup 2026: Suryakumar Yadav called Jasprit Bumrah a "robot" and "national treasure" after his match-winning 4/15 in the T20 World Cup final helped India defend their title against New Zealand.

The Hands of God that shaped Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup redemption Sanju Samson began the year battling doubt and uncertainty. Months later, he stood as India's T20 World Cup hero, lifted by belief, mentorship and a support system that helped him rediscover the fearless batter within.

3 Iran women's football team members head home after rejecting Australia asylum Three members of Iran's women's football team who had accepted humanitarian visas in Australia have now chosen to return home. Their departure leaves only three players from the initial group who decided to stay behind.

Rohit Sharma hopeful India will extend cricket dominance: T20 World Cup just a start Rohit Sharma believes India's recent success in ICC tournaments could be the start of a strong phase for the national teams. The India captain praised both men's and women's teams and hopes the winning run continues.

Watch: Real Madrid's Arda Guler ties LaLiga record with stunning 74-yard goal Arda Guler lit up the Santiago Bernabeu with a sensational long-range strike as Real Madrid beat Elche. The Turkish youngster's outrageous effort from distance matched the longest goal ever scored in LaLiga history.

Formula 1 cancel Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix due to Middle East war Formula 1 has cancelled the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix scheduled for April as tensions escalate in West Asia. Safety concerns linked to the Iran conflict forced the sport to drop both races from the 2026 calendar.

Medvedev ends Alcaraz's winning streak with Indian Wells semi-finals win Daniil Medvedev snapped Carlos Alcaraz's unbeaten run in 2026 with a clinical straight-sets win in the Indian Wells semi-finals. The Russian will now meet Jannik Sinner in the title clash after the Italian defeated Alexander Zverev.

Show a little trust: Suryakumar reveals how a phone call changed Ishan Kishan's fateSuryakumar Yadav revealed how a phone call and a little faith helped Ishan Kishan stage a sensational comeback, earning him a spot in India's Men's T20 World Cup squad. Kishan capitalised on the opportunity, scoring 317 runs in nine matches.

Kuldeep Yadav marries longtime girlfriend Vanshika in a grand Mussoorie ceremonyIndian cricketer Kuldeep Yadav married his childhood friend, Vanshika Singh, on March 14 in a grand ceremony held in Mussoorie. The celebrations were even more special for Kuldeep, coming shortly after India's T20 World Cup 2026 triumph.

New Zealand series gave enough petrol in my tank to go extra mile: Sanju SamsonSpeaking at the India Today Conclave, Sanju Samson credited the T20I series against New Zealand for preparing him to succeed on the global stage. After scoring just 46 runs across five games in the bilateral series, Samson went on to earn the Player of the Tournament award at the T20 World Cup.

Pakistan needs to move on from scapegoat culture: National selectorPakistan national selector Aaqib Javed believes that the country and the country's cricket board need to move on from scapegoating culture to move forward in international cricket.

Surya, Gambhir and Shah visit Siddhivinayak temple with ICC trophySuryakumar Yadav, Gautam Gambhir and Jay Shah carried India's T20 World Cup trophy to Mumbai's Siddhivinayak Temple. Earlier, they had also taken the trophy to a Hanuman temple in Ahmedabad after India's title triumph.

Abhishek Sharma recalls father backing him amid poor form: He tried puja and paathSpeaking at the India Today Conclave in New Delhi, Abhishek Sharma revealed how his father and family helped him overcome a difficult T20 World Cup campaign, during which he battled poor form before scoring a match-winning 18-ball fifty in the final against New Zealand.

Get married, bhai: Indian cricketers jealous of Abhishek's fandom, jokes SamsonIndian cricketers were slightly jealous of Abhishek Sharma's popularity, joked Sanju Samson at the India Today Conclave 2026. At the event, Samson engaged in some hilarious leg-pulling, asking Abhishek to get married soon.

Not ice and fire, we are fire and fire: Samson on partnership with Abhishek SharmaIndia batter Sanju Samson had a hilarious quip about Abhishek Sharma after their T20 World Cup 2026 win. Speaking on their opening partnership, Samson said that it was not ice and fire, it was fire and fire.

MS Dhoni, Ruturaj, AR Rahman: CSK unveils mega spectacle for fans in ChennaiChennai Super Kings have announced a mega fan event titled ROAR 26 at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on March 22, featuring MS Dhoni, Ruturaj Gaikwad, CSK legends and a live performance by AR Rahman.

Kevin Pietersen steps down as Delhi Capitals mentor before IPL 2026Kevin Pietersen has stepped down as Delhi Capitals mentor ahead of IPL 2026, saying he could not commit the time required, though he confirmed he will return to the commentary box during the tournament.

Pakistan cricketers not fined after T20 World Cup poor show: PCB dismisses reportsThe PCB has denied reports that Pakistan players were fined PKR 5 million each for their T20 World Cup exit, with spokesperson Amir Mir clarifying that no penalties have been imposed, though the board is considering a performance-based accountability formula.

Kaif takes Salman Agha's side on run-out controversy, gets schooled by fansFormer India cricketer Mohammad Kaif sided with Salman Agha over the Pakistan batter's run out in the 2nd ODI against Bangladesh. Soon, he got schooled by cricket fans about the spirit of cricket.

Jasprit Bumrah is the Usman Tariq of fast bowlers: Aaqib Javed makes bizarre claimPakistan selector Aaqib Javed triggered debate by comparing Jasprit Bumrah with Usman Tariq. Bumrah finished the T20 World Cup campaign as the joint-highest wicket-taker with 14 scalps and produced a record-setting spell in the final against New Zealand.

Salman Agha gets reprimanded for on-field tantrum after controversial run-outSalman Ali Agha has been given a reprimand and a demerit point following his outburst after the controversial run out during the second ODI against Bangladesh on Friday, March 13.

Badminton: Saina Nehwal strongly opposes proposed 15-point systemFormer Indian badminton star Saina Nehwal has strongly opposed the proposed 15-point system. Saina has said that any changes in the system should be carefully considered.

Trump banned from LA 2028, FIFA World Cup? World Anti-Doping Agency ponders big moveWADA is considering banning Donald Trump and US government officials from the 2028 LA Olympics and FIFA World Cup over USD 7.3 million in unpaid dues in a growing standoff.

Chinese GP: Kimi Antonelli creates history, becomes youngest-ever pole sitterKimi Antonelli made history by taking pole position as a teenager, leading a Mercedes qualifying one-two in Shanghai. George Russell overcame technical issues to secure second, while Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton finished third fastest.

On This Day: Laxman-Dravid heroics power India's Kolkata miracle vs AustraliaOn this day on March 14, 2001, India overcame a massive first-innings deficit to script a remarkable 171-run victory against Australia in the 2001 Kolkata Test, powered by the record-breaking partnership of Rahul Dravid and VVS Laxman and a dominant display by Harbhajan Singh on the final day.

BCCI to honour Rahul Dravid, Roger Binny, Mithali Raj with Lifetime Achievement AwardRahul Dravid, Roger Binny and Mithali Raj headline the BCCI Naman Awards 2026 in New Delhi, as Indian cricket celebrates its legends and a historic run of ICC trophy wins.

Time for MS Dhoni to pass the baton to CSK's next-gen stars in IPL 2026: Irfan PathanIrfan Pathan believes IPL 2026 is the right time for MS Dhoni to hand over responsibilities to CSK's next generation, with Ayush Mhatre, Ruturaj Gaikwad and others ready to step up.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
